---
title: "User accounts/Administrator/Root"
date: 2013-01-28
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by stevesy on 2013-01-28
Hi, after installing Lubuntu 12.10 I set up a standard user account for myself instead of an administrative  one.  From what I understand an administrator account is a root account, which generally shouldn't be used.   Have I understood this  correctly? Also, when you have to authenticate some action in  the gui by giving your login password, is that the equivalent of using  the sudo command in the terminal?  Just trying to get my head around all  of this..   [IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/jpeg;base64,[/IMG] Thanks for any help.

---

### Post by arpanaut on 2013-01-28
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/RootSudo](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/RootSudo)

That gives the basics.
a little more
[http://linux.about.com/od/ubuntu_doc/a/ubudg24t4.htm](http://linux.about.com/od/ubuntu_doc/a/ubudg24t4.htm)
